items.title: A Real-World Game Plan for AI in Renewable Energy
items.description_en: By following a structured approach grounded in operational realities, renewables players can create substantial value from AI. Lessons distilled from BCG’s work with clients can help companies make the most of the technology. Players are struggling to generate value from AI. Pilots are often not scaled effectively, la…
summary: Players are struggling to generate value from AI.
AI can increase worker productivity by 15% to 25% and energy yield by one to three percentage points.
With 10 to 15 use cases, companies typically achieve 60% to 70% of the value potential.
items._summary_source: By following a structured approach grounded in operational realities, renewables players can create substantial value from AI. Lessons distilled from BCG’s work with clients can help companies make the most of the technology. Players are struggling to generate value from AI. Pilots are often not scaled effectively, lack measurable impact, or fail to deal with front-line issues. AI can increase worker productivity by 15% to 25% and energy yield by one to three percentage points. With 10 to 15 use cases, companies typically achieve 60% to 70% of the value potential. Recommendations include focusing on value creation from the outset, solving operational constraints, moving fast but with disciplined governance, and having the right processes and people in place to succeed.
items.chart_image_alt: A Real-World Game Plan for AI in Renewable Energy

Tech-savvy trading firms often allocate 20% to 25% or more of their cost base to technology and reinvest 10% or more of gross margin into platforms and people .

items._summary_source: AI can fundamentally transform how retail banks operate, but only if they fundamentally rewire how work is done. AI-first banks are taking a strategic approach by prioritizing three or four business functions for end-to-end transformation. They are realizing significant benefits from GenAI by reorganizing work so that AI performs 70% to 80% of the toil or repetitive tasks and 30% to 50% of the reasoning tasks.

items.title: Patients Are Sending Digital Signals. Are Companies Listening?
items.description_en: Great marketing tools are meaningless without the right data and technology to personalize patient interactions. Legacy direct-to-patient programs often don’t meet prospective patient expectations for accessibility and ease of use, and they follow predetermined steps regardless of what the patient is doing. AI tools t…
summary: Great marketing tools are meaningless without the right data and technology to personalize patient interactions.
Legacy direct-to-patient programs often don’t meet prospective patient expectations for accessibility and ease of use
BCG가 짚은 핵심 쟁점과 영향 포인트를 함께 확인할 수 있습니다.
items._summary_source: Great marketing tools are meaningless without the right data and technology to personalize patient interactions. Legacy direct-to-patient programs often don’t meet prospective patient expectations for accessibility and ease of use, and they follow predetermined steps regardless of what the patient is doing. AI tools that connect prospective patients’ fragmented information across the health care organization can identify what people need in real time and, crucially, incorporate patient outcomes into the system so that it continuously improves. Health care organizations can take straightforward steps to improve direct-to-patient conversion by evaluating current patient pathways and unifying around the most successful ones, then deploying an agentic engine to orchestrate and continuously improve the system.
items.chart_image_alt: Patients Are Sending Digital Signals. Are Companies Listening?

items.title: Trauma’s toll on the workforce
items.description_en: Employees who report experiencing trauma show lower levels of adaptability, learning, engagement, and other work outcomes.
